target.name: EvolutionaryScale
target.founder: Alexander Rives, Tom Sercu, Salvatore Candido

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $142M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
EvolutionaryScale is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Alexander Rives, Tom Sercu, and Salvatore Candido that has raised $142M to date. The Biotech & Health sector includes 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers, though narrow peer matching within the seed stage proved insufficient for reliable median-based valuation.

Of the three valuation methods assessed, only stage-progression analysis was applicable. Using the $142M total capital raised as a proxy for seed-stage value, a 2.5x multiplier for progression to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $355M. Peer-median analysis was inapplicable due to an inadequate peer set at the same or adjacent stage with similar valuation characteristics (fewer than 3 qualifying datapoints). Public-comp anchoring could not be applied because EvolutionaryScale's revenue is not disclosed, precluding P/S multiple application despite a median 5.8x P/S ratio across the three public biotech comparables.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $302M to $408M, centered on $355M. This range reflects the inherent uncertainty of relying on a single valuation method and the absence of revenue data that would enable cross-validation through public market multiples. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x stage multiplier for this specific company and underlying market conditions for seed-stage biotech valuations.
